Adversarial reproduction: p8-023-gguf-numtensor-uncapped
Environment: darwin/arm64, go test harness calling fs/ggml.Decode directly
Commit: 57653b8e (HEAD at verification time)

Test was placed at fs/ggml/adversarial_repro_test.go, executed, then removed.
A copy is preserved in this directory for review.

Run: go test -v ./fs/ggml/ -run 'TestAdvMaliciousGGUF_(Baseline|1M|10M)$' -count=1

Results:

=== RUN   TestAdvMaliciousGGUF_Baseline
    numTensor=100 blob size: 0.00 MB
    numTensor=100 err=tensor "" offset+size (2436) exceeds file size (2424)
    elapsed=209.917µs peak_heap=0.00 MB delta_heap=0.06 MB total_allocs=449

=== RUN   TestAdvMaliciousGGUF_1M
    numTensor=1000000 blob size: 22.89 MB
    numTensor=1000000 err=tensor "" offset+size (24000036) exceeds file size (24000024)
    elapsed=101.821875ms peak_heap=165.56 MB delta_heap=134.47 MB total_allocs=4000076

=== RUN   TestAdvMaliciousGGUF_10M
    numTensor=10000000 blob size: 228.88 MB
    numTensor=10000000 err=tensor "" offset+size (240000036) exceeds file size (240000024)
    elapsed=1.090400083s peak_heap=1575.54 MB delta_heap=1319.16 MB total_allocs=40000074

Analysis:
- Blob amplification ratio ~6.9x (input bytes -> peak heap allocation before failure).
- All allocation (Tensor struct + slice backing array) occurs inside the
  for range llm.numTensor() loop at fs/ggml/gguf.go:194-233 BEFORE any
  file-size bound check runs. The bound check at lines 258-262 runs only
  after the full loop completes.
- The bound check then rejects the file, but by then 10M Tensor pointers
  have already been allocated (1.5 GB peak RSS).
- GC was disabled during the test (debug.SetGCPercent(-1)) to measure
  true peak, representative of what a burst attack causes before GC
  pressure kicks in.

Minimum tensor-info record size on disk = 24 bytes:
   uint64 name-length (0) + uint32 dims (0) + uint32 kind + uint64 offset

Per-tensor heap cost observed ~150 bytes (Tensor{} + slice pointer + heap
bookkeeping + slice growth doubling). Attack amplification factor ~6x.

Attack surface: /api/create references a blob via digest. Unauthenticated
by default. Blob upload endpoint (/api/blobs/:digest) has no size cap.

VERDICT: Confirmed. The vulnerability is reproducible and the protection
brief (file-size bound) only limits the maximum amplification; it does not
prevent uncapped allocation inside the loop.
